next_action: In the OpenLane configuration, set GLB_RESIZER_ALLOW_SETUP_VIOS=1 so that the post-GRT repair_timing -hold call is invoked with -allow_setup_violations. As the resizer_routing_timing.tcl script shows, without that flag hold repair is constrained by -setup_margin 0.025; since the prior repair_timing -setup pass already left setup WNS at -5.67 ns / TNS -1944.95 ns (RSZ-0062 'Unable to repair all setup violations'), every candidate hold-fixing delay-buffer (dlygate4sd3) is rejected because it would push the already-violating setup path further negative, so 5 hold endpoints remain (worst -0.36 ns on core_dmem_req_ack -> _7275_). Enabling -allow_setup_violations lets hold repair converge; the residual setup problem (driven here by an over-loaded CTS with 24 max-fanout violations on clkbuf_leaf_*) should be tackled separately by relaxing the clock period or rerunning CTS with tighter max_fanout, but the immediate flow-level switch that unblocks hold closure is GLB_RESIZER_ALLOW_SETUP_VIOS=1.
